questions.question: Explain why ECC can achieve the same security level as RSA with smaller key sizes.
expectedPoints: The security of ECC relies on the Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem (ECDLP)
The best known attacks on ECDLP have exponential complexity
Best known attacks on RSA have sub-exponential complexity
This allows ECC to provide equivalent security with smaller parameters

questions.question: Describe the complete process of ECDH key exchange, including the mathematical operations involved.
expectedPoints: Both parties agree on public parameters (curve E, base point G)
Each party generates a private key (random integer) and computes their public key (scalar multiplication)
Parties exchange public keys over potentially insecure channel
Each party computes the shared secret using their private key and the other's public key
Mathematical explanation of why both arrive at the same shared secret
Discussion of security properties and assumptions
